title: srs-to-jira tasks.json
description: Contract between the mapper (phase 1) and the diff/gate (phase 2). Written only by build_tasks.py; never hand-edited.

skip_nfr.description: Recorded so the diff does NOT report filtered-out NFRs as orphaned issues.

model_sha256.description: Digest of the source project-model.json. The map binds to it so two different SRS cannot share one Jira project map.

deprecated_ids.description: Entities with status=deprecated. Not ticketed. Distinct from orphaned: the SRS retired them on purpose.

warnings.description: Surfaced in the approval gate so the reviewer sees thin requirements BEFORE the tickets exist.

kind.description: tbd = a <TBD: ...> placeholder was dropped; missing = field absent; empty_body = nothing to put in the issue.

source_id.description: Join key across tasks.json, task-breakdown.md and jira-map.json.

priority.description: Already mapped to the JIRA name. `Mid` (a ProjectModel value) must never appear here.

labels.description: Always ['morkit-srs', 'morkit-id-<SOURCE_ID>']. Recovery strips the 'morkit-id-' prefix to recover source_id, so the prefix is type-neutral and MUST NOT be changed.

source_hash.description: sha256(summary + NUL + description) of the MACHINE-RENDERED content. Drives the diff. Distinct from jira-map.json's pushed_hash, which records what was actually sent — keeping them separate is what stops a reviewer's hand-edit from being reverted on the next run.
